---
title: "Tarea 4 – Interpolación polinomial y splines"
author: "Luis Manuel Duran"
format: html
execute: 
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy.interpolate import lagrange, CubicSpline
from scipy.stats import chi2
import pandas as pd
import yfinance as yf
import plotly.graph_objects as go


#EJERCICIO 1 – BASES DE LAGRANGE

x=np.arange(6)
y=np.array([1,3,-1,2,0,4])   # valores genéricos

def L(k,xp):
    prod=1
    for j in range(len(x)):
        if j!=k:
            prod*= (xp-x[j])/(x[k]-x[j])
    return prod

xx=np.linspace(0,5,300)
plt.figure()
for k in range(len(x)):
    plt.plot(xx,[L(k,t) for t in xx],label=f"L{k}")
plt.legend(); plt.title("Bases de Lagrange"); plt.grid()

P=lagrange(x,y)
plt.figure()
plt.scatter(x,y)
plt.plot(xx,P(xx))
plt.title("Polinomio interpolante")
plt.grid()


#EJERCICIO 2 – LAGRANGE VS SPLINES

#a) f(x)=x cos x -3x
f=lambda x:x*np.cos(x)-3*x
df=lambda x:np.cos(x)-x*np.sin(x)-3

xp=np.array([0,0.5,1,1.5])
yp=f(xp)
P=lagrange(xp,yp)
cs_nat=CubicSpline(xp,yp,bc_type='natural')
cs_cond=CubicSpline(xp,yp,bc_type=((1,df(0)),(1,df(1.5))))

x0=0.8
print("Lagrange:",P(x0),P.deriv()(x0))
print("Spline nat:",cs_nat(x0),cs_nat(x0,1))
print("Spline cond:",cs_cond(x0),cs_cond(x0,1))

#b) f(x)=log(e^x+2)
f=lambda x:np.log(np.exp(x)+2)
df=lambda x:np.exp(x)/(np.exp(x)+2)

xp=np.array([-2,-1,0,1,2])
yp=f(xp)
P=lagrange(xp,yp)
cs=CubicSpline(xp,yp,bc_type='natural')

print(P(0.5),cs(0.5))

#c) f(x)=e^x+e^{-x}
f=lambda x:np.exp(x)+np.exp(-x)
df=lambda x:np.exp(x)-np.exp(-x)

xp=np.array([0,0.5,1,1.5,2])
yp=f(xp)
P=lagrange(xp,yp)
cs=CubicSpline(xp,yp)

print(P(0.75),cs(0.75))


#EJERCICIO 3 – DENSIDAD DEL AGUA

T=np.array([50,60,65,75,80])
rho=np.array([988,985.7,980.5,974.8,971.6])

P=lagrange(T,rho)
cs=CubicSpline(T,rho)

print("63°C Lagrange:",P(63))
print("63°C spline:",cs(63))


#EJERCICIO 4 – SPLINE MANUAL

np.random.seed(123)
x=np.arange(0,11)
y=np.random.choice(np.arange(-25,26),len(x))
cs=CubicSpline(x,y)


#EJERCICIO 5 – SPLINES CONDICIONADOS (PLOTLY)

def spline_plot(f,a,b,n):
    x=np.linspace(a,b,n+1)
    y=f(x)
    cs=CubicSpline(x,y)
    xx=np.linspace(a,b,400)
    fig=go.Figure()
    fig.add_scatter(x=xx,y=f(xx),name="Real")
    fig.add_scatter(x=xx,y=cs(xx),name="Spline")
    fig 

spline_plot(lambda x:np.exp(-x)*np.sin(np.pi*x),0,2,19)


#EJERCICIO 6 – SPLINES CHI-CUADRADA

k=10
f=lambda x:chi2.pdf(x,k)

def spline_chi(n):
    x=np.linspace(0,30,n+1)
    cs=CubicSpline(x,f(x))
    xx=np.linspace(0,30,500)
    fig=go.Figure()
    fig.add_scatter(x=xx,y=f(xx),name="Real")
    fig.add_scatter(x=xx,y=cs(xx),name=f"Spline n={n}")
    display(fig)

for n in [5,7,10,15,20]:
    spline_chi(n)

#EJERCICIO 7 – INDICADORES INEGI (EJEMPLO)

# Ejemplo simulando datos mensuales
fechas=pd.date_range("2022-01-01","2023-12-01",freq="ME")
indicador1=np.sin(np.linspace(0,6,len(fechas)))+10
indicador2=np.cos(np.linspace(0,6,len(fechas)))+20

cs1=CubicSpline(range(len(indicador1)),indicador1)
cs2=CubicSpline(range(len(indicador2)),indicador2)




#EJERCICIO 8 – ACCIONES YFINANCE

acciones=yf.download(["AAPL","MSFT"],period="2y",interval="1mo")["Close"]
acciones=acciones.dropna()

x=np.arange(len(acciones))
csA=CubicSpline(x,acciones["AAPL"])
cs
M=CubicSpline(x,acciones["MSFT"])
